commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: For mine eyes have seen thy salvation. "Salvation," in Greek ÏƒÏ‰Ï„Î®Î¶Î¹Î¿Î½, the word used by the Septuagint as a rendering of the Hebrew ×™×©×•×¦×—, iescua, safety. "Safety" is used by metonomy for "Saviour." By "salvation," then, we are to understand the Saviour Christ, whom the ancient fathers desired to see, but Simeon alone saw, touched, and embraced.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Thy salvation; i.e. the Saviour, whom thou hast sent. (Witham)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
